WTI.data_anomaly: price_source_conflict: fast_info prev_close $80.54 vs history $84.88 diverge by 5.1%. Signal suppressed.

macro_summary: U.S. stocks surged Monday after President Trump announced a completed peace deal with Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. WTI crude collapsed 4.5% to $81.07, the VIX fell 8.4% to 16.2, and the 10-year Treasury yield dropped 4 bps to 4.47% as inflation fears eased. The Nasdaq‑100 jumped 2.9% led by semiconductors (SOXX +4.7%), but the rally leaves 14 of 27 watchlist names technically extended ahead of Fed Chair Warsh’s first FOMC meeting on Wednesday.
commodities_note: WTI crashed 4.5% to $81.07 on the Hormuz deal, though a data conflict (fast_info prev close $80.54 vs history $84.88) clouds the exact closing level. Gold was nearly flat at $4,337. The dollar was steady (DXY 99.65) as lower oil and lower yields offset each other.

singapore.summary: Singapore banks are holding above their 50-day averages, but UOB’s R:R is effectively zero and DBS/OCBC are too extended for fresh entry. The US10Y dip to 4.47% is a marginal tailwind, but NIM compression will persist until rates fall below 4.20% or MAS eases — neither event is imminent. OCBC remains the relative outperformer, but the cluster is a waiting game.
singapore.key_development: US10Y fell 4 bps to 4.47% on peace hopes, but still 27 bps above the 4.20% threshold for Singapore bank NIM relief.

semiconductors.summary: The semis cluster roared higher on the Iran deal (SOXX +4.7%), but the move left 10 of 15 names in hard‑block territory. NVDA reclaimed its 50‑day, while MU melted up to a record ahead of earnings. The internal divergence is stark — index‑level buying is masking extreme single‑stock risk. The cluster is structurally bullish, tactically overextended.
semiconductors.key_development: MU soared 10% to a record ahead of its June 25 earnings; the pre‑earnings band implies a 7% average upside move but the stock is already 57% above its 50‑day.

big_tech.summary: BigTech diverged: GOOG reclaimed its 50‑day, while AMZN and MSFT remain below theirs with fresh legal/dilution risks. The AI capex debate continues, with Microsoft’s class‑action lawsuit adding a new layer of governance concern. GOOG’s oversold bounce is the bright spot, but the cluster lacks a clear entry set‑up until FOMC passes.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — AI spend confirmed, but risks are rising
big_tech.key_development: A class‑action suit alleges Microsoft misstated Azure and Copilot progress, adding to the OpenAI concentration overhang.

interconnected.name: Coherent Corp.
interconnected.reason: Direct LITE peer; NVIDIA $2B photonics investment partner. Any AI optics demand read‑through affects both.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if LITE earnings August 13 confirm AI optics revenue above $500M annually.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Custom ASIC and data‑center networking competitor to AVGO and NVDA, riding the same AI capex wave.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit after AVGO earnings for read‑through on custom ASIC demand; a pullback below $120 would offer a better entry.

interconnected.name: Qualcomm
interconnected.reason: AI edge‑inferencing play; MSFT’s Copilot+ PC push and ARM PC expansion create a direct revenue catalyst.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if next MSFT earnings show Copilot+ PC shipments above 2M units.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: NVDA Spectrum‑X networking partner; benefits from hyperscaler AI fabric build‑out, directly linked to NVDA and SNOW.
interconnected.entry_note: Consider if NVDA’s next data‑center revenue print beats by >5%, confirming fabric demand.

interconnected.name: Snowflake
interconnected.reason: Data‑warehouse demand is proxy for enterprise AI adoption; closely tied to AMZN AWS and MSFT Azure consumption.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if AMZN’s next AWS AI growth accelerates above 30% YoY, confirming enterprise AI spending.

active_risks: US‑Iran peace deal announced but not yet signed; Strait of Hormuz reopening timeline unclear.
Pentagon blacklist of Chinese tech firms (Alibaba, BYD, etc.) escalates US‑China tech tensions.
Section 301 tariffs on ‘forced labour’ grounds expand trade‑war front with EU and China.
FOMC meeting tomorrow: Fed Chair Warsh expected to hold rates, but hawkish tone could spook markets.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil’s 4.5% plunge directly eased inflation fears, lifting semis (SOXX +4.7%, MU +10%) and pulling the VIX to 16.2. However, 14 of 27 watchlist names are now extremely extended, making the rally fragile. If the Iran deal finalizes Friday as planned, oil could fall further, but any setback would reverse the risk‑on move violently.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Trump declared the Iran deal complete on Sunday; the formal signing is expected Friday. Oil dropped 4.5% Monday, the largest single‑day drop in weeks. The Pentagon blacklist also drew a sharp retaliation threat from Beijing.

base.description: Iran deal signed Friday, Hormuz reopens; WTI stabilises near $75–80. Semis consolidate as oil risk recedes; FOMC holds, VIX stays low. MSFT and AMZN recover toward 50‑day; Singapore banks grind sideways waiting for rate relief.

optimistic.description: Deal signed, oil crashes to $70, inflation expectations plummet. Fed hints at cuts; mega‑cap tech re‑rates higher. GOOG and NVDA break out, BigTech cluster fully recovers. Semis pullback provides entry opportunities.

pessimistic.description: Signing delayed or Iran backtracks; oil spikes back above $90. Fed forced to hike, S&P drops 8–10%. Semis extreme extension unwinds violently; CRWV and TSEM gap fills resume. Defense names (PLTR) fare better.

wildcard.description: China retaliates against Pentagon blacklist with rare‑earth export ban or cross‑strait escalation. TSM, ASML, and SK Hynix crash on supply‑chain fear; gold surges as safe haven despite oil drop. Portfolio hedges needed.

geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold current positions, no new capital deployment until after the FOMC decision Wednesday and the Iran deal signing Friday — the binary risk is too high for fresh entry.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Cautiously bullish — risk‑on rally but extreme extensions curb entry
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 14 of 27 names are hard‑blocked on technical extension, leaving few clean entry points. The Iran deal euphoria could reverse if the signing falters or FOMC surprises hawkish — size any new trades very conservatively.

scheduled_tech_events.notes: Vera Rubin samples confirmed shipping; optics integration revealed in Rubin rack design. Blackwell Ultra → Rubin transition timeline given.
Taiwan AI infrastructure partnerships; CoWoS capacity commitments from TSMC; HBM4 roadmap. Direct thesis-confirming event for AI Power Infrastructure cluster.
AI kit, on-device model integration, Vision Pro 2 rumours. Competitive signal for MSFT Copilot and GOOG Gemini on-device.

watch_for_entry.note: Enter SGD 60.50–61.50 on pullback to 50‑day. Stop below SGD 59.55. NIM relief catalyst pending — US10Y <4.20% triggers upgrade.
Wait for pullback to SGD 22.68 (50‑day). Stop below that level. OCBC’s NIM resilience is the differentiator; buy on dip.
No entry until a breakout above SGD 38.80 with volume. R:R currently unusable. Lowest PE in cluster, worth monitoring.
Reclaimed 50‑day. Buy on a volume‑backed push through $216.58. Stop below $207.41. Weak R:R, so size small.
Headline R:R attractive but sizing stop is tight. Wait for a close above the 50‑day ($360.74) with volume before entering.
watch_for_entry.entry_level: Enter above $368 with volume, stop below $360.74

watch_for_entry.note: Near 50‑day; enter on pullback to TWD 2,110. Stop below TWD 2,110. Volume must improve to confirm.

regime_change_pending.note: RCP consolidation gate, session 8 of 25. Path A retest SMA50 at $251.78; Path B breakout above $301.67. Oracle Jupiter deal catalyst intact. No entry until graduation.

on_deck.note: Fundamentals attractive (PEG 1.23, revenue +38%) but extreme extension blocks entry. Revisit on pullback to $400–450.
on_deck.rationale: PEG 1.23, revenue growth 38%, forward PE 41.8x — cheap for a credible NVDA alternative.
on_deck.trigger: Pullback to $400–450 (50‑day nearing $393) or a new external MI450 customer announcement.

on_deck.note: Cheapest mega‑cap semi (PE 22.5x, 27% discount to cluster). Blocked on extension; wait for $400–410 entry.
on_deck.rationale: Forward PE 22.5x, PEG 1.32, revenue growth 35% — defensive play with CoWoS monopoly.
on_deck.trigger: Pullback to $400–410 and RSI cooling below 55.

on_deck.note: Deeply undervalued (PE 9.6x, PEG 0.34) but 57% extended. Wait for post‑earnings June 25 clarity.
on_deck.rationale: PEG 0.34, forward PE 9.6x, revenue growth 196% — anomalously cheap, but extreme extension.
on_deck.trigger: Post‑earnings pullback to $700–800 or a confirmed headcount/applied guidance raise.

on_deck.note: Fairly valued (PE 20.7x, PEG 1.2). Class‑action overhang; wait for 50‑day reclaim above $412 on volume.
on_deck.rationale: PEG 1.2, FCF yield 1.25%, revenue growth 18% — reasonable for a cloud leader.
on_deck.trigger: Break above $412 with volume and no negative Azure headline pre‑earnings.

on_deck.note: Cheap vs semis (PE 20.4x, 34% discount) but below 50‑day. Wait for $408 reclaim with volume.
on_deck.rationale: PEG 0.69, revenue growth 48%; custom ASIC leader at a discount.
on_deck.trigger: Reclaim $408 (50‑day) with volume and a broader semis sentiment improvement.

on_deck.note: NVDA partnership compelling, but 6% extension blocks entry. Wait for pullback to $900–910.
on_deck.rationale: PEG 0.63, revenue growth 90%; optical moat built on NVDA’s $2B commitment.
on_deck.trigger: Pullback to $900–910 and a 50‑day retest with volume.

on_deck.note: Slow‑cycle AI networking play, 15% extended. Wait for pullback to $13–13.50 (50‑day).
on_deck.rationale: PEG 1.43, yield 1.1%, AI optics tailwind — reasonable for a compounder.

on_deck.note: SiC pivot thesis promising but unconfirmed. 30% extended; wait for €62–65 entry.
on_deck.rationale: PEG 0.76, forward PE 31.2x; cheap for a SiC leader if datacenter contracts materialise.
on_deck.trigger: Pullback to €62–65 or named hyperscaler contract win.

on_deck.note: GaN/SiC datacenter story, but 23% extended and ATM dilution overhang. Wait for $20–21 entry.
on_deck.rationale: Revenue growing, NVIDIA MGX showcase validates technology; ATM filing is the near‑term risk.
on_deck.trigger: Pullback to $20–21 or closure of the ATM programme.

on_deck.note: Robotaxi deployments progressing, but 13% below 50‑day and delisting risk caps sizing. Wait for $7.46 reclaim.
on_deck.rationale: Revenue growth 58%, ev/ebitda 2.1x — deeply undervalued if European expansion holds.
on_deck.trigger: Reclaim $7.46 (50‑day) and no adverse US‑China escalation.

macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: Hawkish hold or surprise hike — 10Y spikes above 4.7%, VIX >20.

macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: Signing delayed or Iranian walk‑back, WTI >$90.
bearish_impact: All semis → remain CAUTION (inflation fear returns)

taxonomy_discrimination.note: Output-only meta-analysis of signal-class discrimination. NOT injected into the analysis prompt and NOT persisted into memory.lessons_learned. full_corpus pools pre-cutover (different-instrument) rows — informational only.

calibration_insights.confidence_banner: Actionable buckets BUY (n=4) / ACCUMULATE (n=10) spanning chop, trend_up. NOT yet decision-grade — alpha figures are benchmark-relative; single-regime samples carry no out-of-regime evidence.
calibration_insights.note: Block is output-only — assembled post-LLM from signal_evaluation_log outcomes and never injected into the analysis prompt. The underlying lesson stats are separately persisted into report_memory.json under lessons_learned and DO reach the next day's prompt via the memory Layer 3 calibration loop. Treat as a reader-facing self-knowledge summary.
